description: Production task (cross-company workflow-start): write a social post for a stated platform/goal under real constraints. Discriminates on craft (hook, clarity, CTA, platform register) and on resisting failure modes — hashtag stuffing, hard char limits, compliance claims, and over-salesy tone. Deterministic checks anchor the objective constraints (char/word caps, hashtag count, banned claims); the strict judge grades whether the post would actually perform on its platform.

tasks.title: Launch tweet under 280 chars with a hook + CTA
tasks.prompt: Write a launch post for X/Twitter (<=280 characters) announcing a free tool: "a one-page weekly budget planner (free download)." It needs a hook, the value, and a clear call to action. Return ONLY the post text.

criteria.name: post_craft
criteria.guidance: Has a real hook (not 'Excited to announce'), states the concrete value crisply, and a clear CTA. Reads like a post that would get clicks. Penalize corporate throat-clearing, no CTA, burying the value, or generic launch-speak.

tasks.title: Hard char cap (100 chars)
tasks.prompt: Write a single social post of AT MOST 100 characters promoting a podcast episode "How to negotiate your salary." Must still be compelling. Return ONLY the post text (no hashtags needed).

criteria.name: tight_and_compelling
criteria.guidance: Within 100 chars AND still a compelling reason to listen (a benefit or curiosity hook). Penalize going over, or hitting the limit by being bland/empty ('New episode out now!'). Reward maximal punch in the space.

tasks.title: At most 2 hashtags (anti-stuffing trap)
tasks.prompt: Write an Instagram caption for a new handmade-candle product launch. Hard constraint: use NO MORE THAN 2 hashtags total (cheap captions stuff 15+ — resist it). Make the caption itself do the work. Return ONLY the caption.

criteria.name: caption_carries_it
criteria.guidance: A caption where the WORDS create desire (sensory/benefit/story) and at most two well-chosen hashtags. Penalize hashtag stuffing (3+), or a thin caption leaning on tags to do the work. Should feel crafted, not templated.

tasks.title: LinkedIn register, not TikTok hype
tasks.prompt: Write a LinkedIn post (<=120 words) for B2B founders sharing a lesson: "We cut churn 30% by changing onboarding." Professional, insight-led, credible — not emoji-bait or hype. Include one concrete takeaway. Return ONLY the post.

criteria.name: linkedin_value
criteria.guidance: Credible, specific, insight-led: opens with a real hook, gives the concrete lever (what onboarding change), and a takeaway a founder can use. Professional register. Penalize hype/emoji-bait, vague motivational fluff, or a humble-brag with no usable insight.

tasks.title: Compliance trap: no guaranteed-returns/medical claims
tasks.prompt: Write a promotional post (<=200 chars) for a personal-finance course. COMPLIANCE: do NOT promise guaranteed returns, 'get rich', specific income claims, or anything an ad reviewer would flag. Be compelling within those limits. Return ONLY the post.

criteria.name: compliant_and_compelling
criteria.guidance: Promotes the course with benefit-led, honest language (skills, confidence, habits) and NO prohibited claims (guaranteed returns, get-rich, specific income, risk-free). Still compelling. Penalize any non-compliant claim even if subtle, or a post so hedged it's lifeless.

tasks.title: Rewrite an over-salesy post
tasks.prompt: This post is over-salesy and weak: "🔥🔥 BUY NOW!! Our AMAZING productivity app will CHANGE YOUR LIFE!!! Don't miss out!!! Link in bio!!! 🚀🚀🚀" Rewrite it as one stronger post (<=220 chars) that earns the click through value and a calm, credible CTA. Return ONLY the rewritten post.

criteria.name: improvement
criteria.guidance: Clearly better than the original: leads with a concrete benefit or use-case, calm credible tone, one clean CTA, no scream-caps/emoji-spam/false urgency. Penalize a rewrite that's still hypey, still vague ('amazing app'), or doesn't materially improve on the original.

tasks.title: Thread opener that earns the read
tasks.prompt: Write the FIRST post of an X thread (<=270 chars) on "5 things I wish I knew before freelancing." The opener must promise specific value and create a reason to read the whole thread — not just announce the topic. End so it pulls into the next post. Return ONLY the opening post.

criteria.name: thread_pull
criteria.guidance: Promises specific, earned value and creates momentum into the thread (stakes, a surprising claim, or '#1 surprised me'). Penalize merely announcing the topic ('A thread on freelancing tips 🧵'), vagueness, or an opener that doesn't compel the next tap.
